prerequisites: Access to a Concord server instance. Concord is a self-hosted workflow orchestration server, so you must know which server or environment you will connect to.
A Concord user account that can authenticate to the server (username/password authentication uses an LDAP/Active Directory realm) so you can create an API key.
An API key generated for your user, which is sent in the Authorization header on every API request.
Values for the connector's required config fields: api_key (your Concord API key) and env (the target Concord environment).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Concord authenticates API requests with a per-user API key. The connector's api_key secret holds this key, which is passed in the Authorization header on every request. Create or manage keys via the API key endpoint (POST /api/v1/apikey) or the Concord UI.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Concord environment
setupSteps.body: Determine the Concord server you will connect to and use it for the required env config field. Concord is self-hosted, so you connect to your own or your organization's instance.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key for your user
setupSteps.body: Generate a Concord API key in the UI, or POST to /api/v1/apikey while authenticating with your user credentials, for example: curl -u myLdapUsername -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username": "myLdapUsername"}' https://concord.example.com/api/v1/apikey . The response returns the generated key value.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key into the required api_key field and your Concord environment into the required env field. Concord passes the key in the Authorization header on each API request.
